steps.speaker: 旁白
steps.text: You're picking up a package downstairs when you hear Mrs. Henderson's voice through her door. It is very loud. She's on the phone with her son, Ryan. He used to live in your apartment — 4B. But the rent went up, so he had to move out.
steps.text_cn: 你在楼下取包裹时，听见Henderson太太的声音从门里传出来，很大声。她正在和儿子Ryan通电话。Ryan以前住在你的公寓4B，但房租涨了，所以他不得不搬走。

steps.speaker: Mrs. Henderson
steps.text: I don't care about the money, Ryan. I just want to SEE you...
steps.text_cn: Ryan，我不在乎钱。我只是想见见你……

steps.instruction: "I don't care about the money, Ryan. I just want to SEE you..." — Why does Mrs. Henderson want to see Ryan?
steps.feedback: Right! She's talking to her son, Ryan, and she misses him so much. / 对！她在跟儿子Ryan打电话，她很想他。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'Her son' means 她的儿子. She's on the phone with Ryan, her son. / 'Her son' 是她的儿子。她在跟儿子Ryan通话。

options.label: A
options.text: She wants to get money from Ryan.

options.label: C
options.text: Her husband wants to see Ryan.

steps.speaker: 旁白
steps.text: That evening. Jake messages you privately.
steps.text_cn: 那天晚上，Jake私下给你发消息。

steps.speaker: Jake
steps.text: Is Mrs. H okay? She didn't answer my message today. That never happens.
steps.text_cn: H太太还好吗？她今天没回我消息。这从来没发生过。

steps.speaker: You
steps.text: I heard something. Not sure if I should say.
steps.text_cn: 我听到了一些事。不确定该不该说。

steps.instruction: 请把下列英文与对应的中文意思配对。
steps.speech_text: Match these words: show up, don't push, come in.
steps.feedback: Nice! Jake's advice: don't ask questions, just be there. / 很好！Jake的建议：别追问，去陪着就好。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'Show up' = 去陪陪/出现, 'don't push' = 别追问, 'come in' = 进来。

steps.speaker: 旁白
steps.text: You knock on her door with a cake from the supermarket.
steps.text_cn: 你带着超市买来的蛋糕去敲她的门。

steps.instruction: 旁白：You knock on her door. Mrs. Henderson opens the door. 你该说什么？
steps.feedback: Sweet! 'I brought you something' is a warm way to offer a gift. / 暖心！'I brought you something' 是送东西时很温暖的说法。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! You have cake for her — 'I brought you something' is the natural thing to say. / 你给她带了蛋糕——'I brought you something' 是最自然的说法。

options.label: C
options.text: I need to close the window.

steps.speaker: You
steps.text: I brought you something. It's not homemade — sorry.
steps.text_cn: 我给你带了点东西。不是自己做的，不好意思。

steps.speaker: Mrs. Henderson
steps.text: Oh — come in, dear. Sit with me.
steps.text_cn: 哦，亲爱的，进来吧。陪我坐一会儿。

steps.instruction: "Come in, dear. Sit with me." — What does Mrs. Henderson want you to do?
steps.feedback: Yes! She wants your company — come in and sit. / 对！她想要你的陪伴——进来坐坐。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'Come in' = 进来, 'sit with me' = 陪我坐坐。She wants you to stay. / 她想让你留下来陪她。

steps.speaker: Mrs. Henderson
steps.text: Thank you for coming. You make me think of Ryan sometimes.
steps.text_cn: 谢谢你来看我。有时候你会让我想起Ryan。

steps.speaker: 旁白
steps.text: Next day, Jake texts: "Thank you. What you did worked. 🙏" Then adds: "She's making brunch this weekend... Do you want to come?"
steps.text_cn: 第二天，Jake发来消息："谢谢你。你做的事有用。🙏" 然后又补了一句："她这周末要做早午餐……你想来吗？"

steps.instruction: Mrs. Henderson is sad because her son left. You visit her with cake and watch TV together. You don't ask about the phone call. Is this a good thing to do?
steps.feedback: Exactly! In the US, just 'showing up' for someone is a powerful way to care. / 没错！在美国，'出现在某人身边'本身就是很有力的关心方式。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! Sometimes people don't want to talk — just being there shows you care. / 有时候人们不想说话——在旁边陪着就是关心。
steps.review_word: Yes — sometimes being there is enough

options.label: A
options.text: Yes — sometimes being there is enough

options.label: B
options.text: No — you should ask many questions

steps.instruction: 用下列词块拼出正确句子（含1个干扰块）。
steps.feedback: Well done! You can offer a gift naturally now. / 做得好！你现在能自然地表达带了东西给别人。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! The correct sentence is 'I brought you something.' 'Brought' is past tense of 'bring'. / 正确句子是 'I brought you something.'（我给你带了东西。）'brought'是'bring'的过去式。

steps.instruction: 请跟读：Come in and sit with me.
steps.target_sentence: Come in and sit with me.
steps.answer: Come in and sit with me.
steps.feedback: Warm and clear! You sound welcoming. / 温暖又清晰！听起来很热情好客。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! Try again: COME in and SIT with me. Stress 'come' and 'sit'. / 再试一次：重读COME和SIT。
steps.review_word: Come in and sit with me.
